title: Spotlight | NYU Shanghai
description: Jointly established by New York University and East China Normal University, NYU Shanghai is the third degree-granting campus in NYU global network, joining NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU New York City.
text:
Spotlight
Dialogue with Directors: Pushing the Boundaries of Research Through Interdisciplinary Collaboration“Future solutions will arise from interdisciplinary collaborations.”
Dialogue with Director Brian Hall: Improving the Health of Diverse Populations across the LifespanProfessor Brian Hall shared with us about the center’s growth, focus, and ongoing projects.
What I’m Working On: Stavros Didakis and the Art of Autonomous AI Stavros Didakis on his thought-provoking AI-art installation "Xenoforms" and experiential learning.
Eric Endo’s Exploration of Ferromagnetism and TeachingDr. Eric Endo arrived at NYU Shanghai in 2018 as a postdoctoral fellow in mathematics.
Dialogue with Chen Jian, Director of NYU Shanghai-ECNU Center on Global History, Economy and CultureProfessor Chen Jian shared with us GHEC’s history, mission and vision, and inspiring projects.
Chen Yuxin on CBER: Bridging NYU Shanghai with the Business WorldProfessor Chen Yuxin reflected on CBER’s mission and shared future plans relating to the Qiantan campus.
Professor Tansen Sen on NYU Shanghai’s Center for Global AsiaProfessor Sen talks about NYU Shanghai's Center for Global Asia and hopes for the Center's next decade.
Postdoctoral Fellow Adhira Mangalagiri: Navigating Transnational Thoughts Through LiteratureDr. Adhira Mangalagiri shared her experience and learnings as a Postdoctoral Fellow at CGA.
Postdoctoral Fellow Li Gen: Serving Society Through Mental Health Research Dr. Li Gen shared his research experience and progress as a postdoctoral fellow at CGHE.
What I’m Working On: Pragmatic Solutions with Rodrigo Zeidan Rodrigo Zeidan is a solution seeker.
At the Piano: Award Winning Composer Teaches CompositionAward-winning composer Bright Sheng teaches The Art of Music Composition and Performance to students.
NYU Shanghai Students Document Home through ‘Slow Storytelling’NYU Shanghai students learn the art of 'Slow Storytelling' through the summer course 'Home Stories.'
2022 DURF Summer Projects Feature Interactive Design and Data AnalysisCatch a sneak peek of four selected projects below!
Voices and Faces From the Class of 2026Six members of the class of 2026 told us who they are and what they’re looking forward to at NYU Shanghai.
IMA Students Build Interactive Virtual Worlds in Lockdown, Look Forward to Future ExhibitionAfter a 6-week workshop, students are excited to exhibit the virtual worlds they've built in lockdown.
